title: System and method for power distribution
abstract: A power distribution system including a first power module and a second power module. The first power module including a first power input receiving an input power, a first transformer receiving the input power and outputting a transformed power, a first power output configured to output the input power, a second power output configured to output the transformed power, and a pass-through output configured to output input power. The second power module including a third power input receiving the input power, from the pass-through output of the first power module, a fourth transformer receiving the input power and outputting the transformed power, a first power output configured to output the input power, and a second power output configured to output the transformed power.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A power distribution system comprising: a first power module including a first power input receiving an input power, a first transformer receiving the input power and outputting a transformed power, a first power output configured to output the input power, a second power output configured to output the transformed power, and a pass-through output configured to output input power; and a second power module including a third power input receiving the input power, from the pass-through output of the first power module, a fourth transformer receiving the input power and outputting the transformed power, a first power output configured to output the input power, and a second power output configured to output the transformed power. 2. The power distribution system of claim 1, wherein the input power is approximately 120 volts alternating-current power and the transformed power is approximately 220 volts alternating-current power. 4. The power distribution system of claim 1, wherein the input power is approximately 220 volts alternating-current power and the transformed power is approximately 120 volts alternating-current power. 5. The power distribution system of claim 1, wherein the first and second transformers are step up and down transformers. 6. The power distribution system of claim 1, wherein at least one selected from the group consisting of the first power module and the second power module further includes a circuit breaker configured to provide over current protection. 7. The power distribution system of claim 1, wherein the first power module further includes a fifth power output. 8. The power distribution system of claim 7 wherein the fifth power output is configured to output a rectified direct-current power. 9. A method of distributing power, the method comprising: receiving, via an input power, an input power; transforming, via a first transformer, the input power into a transformed power; outputting, via a first power output, the input power; outputting, via a second power output, the transformed power; outputting, via a pass-through output, the input power; receiving, via a second power input, the input power from the pass-through output; transforming, via a second transformer, the input power from third power input into a second transformed power; outputting, via a third power output, the input power from the third power input; and outputting, via a fourth power output, the transformed power from the second transformer. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the input power is approximately 120 volts alternating- current power and the transformed power is approximately 220 volts alternating-current power. 1 1. The method of claim 1, wherein the input power is approximately 220 volts alternating- current power and the transformed power is approximately 120 volts alternating-current power. 12. The method of claim 1, wherein the first and second transformers are step up and down transformers. 13. The method of claim 1 , further comprising providing, via a ciruict breaker, over current protection. 14. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: rectifying the input power to a rectified power; and outputting the rectified power.
